purpose: Language-neutral manifest for semantic runtime-core checks that every AgentLedger runtime implementation must report before claiming parity. This file is the authority consumed by the aggregate parity runner; per-language CLIs may execute the checks natively, but they must report these ids.

required_semantic_checks.description: Create a durable run, commit state under a valid lease, export evidence, and replay without side effects.
Persist a completed run to the dependency-free local store, reopen it, and prove final state, evidence, Tool Ledger, and replay remain intact.
Write, read, and validate stable content-addressed refs in the dependency-free local blob store.
Validate ToolSpec input/output schemas at the runtime boundary before treating a tool call as successful.
Run local worker/service loops until terminal, idle, and recovered-lease outcomes are reported correctly.
Retry a side-effecting tool call through the Tool Ledger and prove the external action executes once.
Enforce policy denial, human approval pause/resume, and sandbox fail-closed behavior at the runtime boundary.
Attribute model/tool cost, enforce budgets in flight, and classify failures by source and retryability.
Record media artifact refs and event-stream checkpoints as replayable evidence without owning codec processing or stream transport.
Export language-neutral trace spans, evidence diffs, divergence dimensions, and static debug summaries from evidence bundles.
Run a one-function hello-world agent through the minimal simple API and prove output, state, and result event are recorded.
Translate evidence trace spans into dependency-free OTLP/JSON with resource, scope, spans, and original AgentLedger identifiers.
Export a dependency-free static HTML debug artifact containing run metadata, event timeline, and final state.
Produce read-only retention plans and backup readiness reports from evidence without destructive cleanup.
Expose language-neutral storage schema metadata and DDL helpers for SQLite and Postgres dialects.
Map dependency-free MCP-style tool descriptors and context resources into runtime tool specs.
Expose dependency-free function and method framework adapter base contracts without importing framework SDKs.
Detect direct shell/network/model/tool calls that bypass runtime policy, ledger, sandbox, audit, and replay boundaries while honoring explicit line ignores.
Expose a scheduler facade for runtime-owned status inspection, lease recovery, and cancellation without owning distributed execution backends.
Run side-effect-free adversarial review checks over evidence bundles for release-readiness blockers, approvals, event sequence, durable refs, and optional cost limits.
Run side-effect-free evidence health and regression checks over evidence bundles without embedding a full eval platform in runtime core.
Run executable dependency-free reliability probes for retry exhaustion, stale lease fencing, cancellation fencing, and Tool Ledger side-effect idempotency.
Expose dependency-free shadow report and state diff semantics; full side-effect-replay ShadowRunner remains runtime/adapter-specific.
Expose built-in golden evidence and side-effect-free regression checks for repro harnesses; file-backed corpus UX may remain language/package specific.
Reconstruct a time-travel timeline and state-at-sequence from evidence events, and export dependency-free static HTML.
Expose language-neutral optional adapter capability descriptors for backend, sandbox, framework, MCP transport, and shadow-runner extension boundaries without making heavy SDKs part of runtime core.
Verify official optional adapter contracts through injected clients and dry-run manifests without importing cloud SDKs or requiring local daemons in runtime-core.

reporting_contract.rule: Every runtime conformance CLI must return passed=true and include every required semantic check id in its checks array.
